question_text: Which of the following best explains why a gas exerts pressure on the walls of its container, according to the particle model?

options.label: A
options.text: Gas particles have negative charge that repels the container walls.

options.label: B
options.text: Gas particles are constantly moving in random directions and colliding with the container walls; each collision exerts a small force, and the total of all collisions creates pressure.

options.label: C
options.text: Gas particles stick to the container walls and their weight pushes down on the surface.

options.label: D
options.text: The nucleus of each gas atom releases energy that pushes outward against the container.

explanation: Option B is correct because it applies the kinetic particle model: gas particles move rapidly and randomly, hitting container walls constantly. Each collision transfers momentum (force), and billions of collisions per second create measurable pressure. The other options contain common misconceptions: gases aren't charged (A), particles don't stick (C), and nuclei don't release pressure (D).
